title: [PS-69532] Moved DrawingFDEF POM to inherit from FDEFBase
body:
## Changes:
* The primary change was to move `DrawingFDEF` from inheriting from `FullDefBase` to `FDEFBase` after it has been converted to angular. As `WebViewerBase` inherited from `FullDefBase`, I had to rework the structure of `WebViewerBase`. _Structural changes..._
  * Created `WebViewer` POM
  * Modified `WebViewerBase` to implement `IWebViewer` which is a slim wrapper around `WebViewer` POM functionality which can be used by `FDEFBase` FDEFs (Drawing and DrawingComparison)
  * Created `WebViewerFullDefBase` to implement `IWebViewer` which is a slim wrapper around `WebViewer` POM functionality which can be used by `FullDefBase` FDEFs (Specs, files, photos, etc)
* Updated a bunch of locators
* Added Gainsight IDs to 2 drawing popups for some of the POMs to hook into

_Note: I will have another PR that changes the name `WaitForPageToLoad()` to `IsOnPage()` for correctness as it now returns a bool_

_Note 2: since this switches the drawing URL from the legacy viewer to the new viewer, this may actually increase the number of failures due to bugs in the new viewer, but the failures would now be valid rather than test-only_
author: henrykaus

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Refactors the ParallelBrowserTests Page Object Model (POM) hierarchy so the Angular-migrated `DrawingFDEF` can inherit from `FDEFBase` (instead of `FullDefBase`), while preserving shared Apryse WebViewer interactions via a new `WebViewer` control and lightweight wrappers for both `FDEFBase` and `FullDefBase` pages. Also adds stable Gainsight/QA IDs to drawing popups and updates many locators/tests to match the Angular DOM.

**Changes:**
- Introduced a shared `WebViewer` control + `IWebViewer` contract, and split wrappers into `WebViewerBase` (for `FDEFBase`) and `WebViewerFullDefBase` (for `FullDefBase`).
- Migrated `DrawingFDEF` to the new `FDEFBase`-based viewer model and updated many test locators/usages accordingly.
- Added Gainsight/QA IDs for `measurement-info` and `annotation-edit-dialog` and updated test selectors to use the new DOM.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 37 out of 37 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 38 out of 38 changed files in this pull request and generated 9 comments.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 38 out of 38 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: This makes `URL` not required since some FDEFs don't have URLs (like drawing comparison)
review_comments.author: henrykaus

review_comments.body: `PhotoAnnotationPopup` is locating toolbar buttons via `FindButton("Delete")` / `FindButton("View all")`, but the Angular template for `photo-annotation-popup` uses nomenclature-driven labels (and the delete button has no inner text). These text-based locators are likely to be brittle or fail when the rendered label differs (localization) or when the button is icon-only. Prefer stable CSS hooks that exist in the template (e.g., `.photo-toolbar-button` and `.view-all-button`).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `IsOnPage()` returns true based only on the title. `WaitForPageToLoad()` doesn’t enforce success (it ignores the `Exists()` return value), so this can report true even if the WebViewer never finished loading. Include an explicit WebViewer element existence check in the return condition.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Fixed
review_comments.author: henrykaus

review_comments.body: Updated
review_comments.author: henrykaus

review_comments.body: `URL` is now a virtual auto-property with no default value; if a derived FDEF forgets to override it, `NavigateTo()` will throw at runtime when `string.Format(URL, ...)` runs. Consider making the default implementation throw a clear exception (or keep `URL` abstract) so failures are immediate and actionable.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This close control is an `<i>` with a click handler, which isn’t keyboard-accessible and has no accessible name for screen readers. Prefer a real `<button>` (or at least add keyboard handlers + an aria-label sourced from nomenclature) for the close action.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Icon-only button has no accessible name (no `aria-label`/`title`), so screen readers will not announce its purpose. Add an aria-label/title sourced from nomenclature/settings (e.g. "Open in new tab").
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Icon-only button has no accessible name (no `aria-label`/`title`), so screen readers will not announce its purpose. Add an aria-label/title sourced from nomenclature/settings (e.g. "Open in side panel").
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Icon-only button has no accessible name (no `aria-label`/`title`), so screen readers will not announce its purpose. Add an aria-label/title sourced from nomenclature/settings (e.g. "Open in new tab").
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Clickable thumbnail button has no accessible name. Add an aria-label/title (from nomenclature/settings) describing the action (e.g. "Open in side panel").
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Icon-only button has no accessible name (no `aria-label`/`title`), so screen readers will not announce its purpose. Add an aria-label/title sourced from nomenclature/settings (e.g. "Open in new tab").
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Clickable thumbnail button has no accessible name. Add an aria-label/title (from nomenclature/settings) describing the action (e.g. "Open in side panel").
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Icon-only button has no accessible name (no `aria-label`/`title`), so screen readers will not announce its purpose. Add an aria-label/title sourced from nomenclature/settings (e.g. "Open in new tab").
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This is not a problem for this PR
review_comments.author: henrykaus

review_comments.body: `ViewAllButton` is using `FindButton("button[title='View all']")`, but `FindButton(...)` locates by button caption/locator, not CSS. This will likely never match and will make photo-annotation tests fail/flaky. Use `FindCss(...)` (or another non-localized stable attribute) for this selector.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `AnnotationsPath` exposes public fields `x`/`y`, which violates the C# naming rule for public members (PascalCase) and makes the API inconsistent. Prefer `X`/`Y` as public getters and keep the lowercase members non-public for any existing in-assembly call sites.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `WaitForPageToLoad()` now returns `bool`, but the result is ignored here (previously this was asserted). If the alignment viewer fails to load, this test may continue and fail later with a less actionable error. Assert the return value so failures point at the load step.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Fixed (naming will be fixed in future PR)
review_comments.author: henrykaus

review_comments.body: Fixed
review_comments.author: henrykaus

review_comments.body: Fixed
review_comments.author: henrykaus
